# Open Source Europe

## Open Source Europe

- [Welcome to Open Source Europe](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/master.md): Open Source Europe is a Belgian Association (ASBL/vzw) with a tech platform that enables over 400 open source groups to raise and spend more than 1 million euros annually, all in full transparency.
- [About Open Source Europe](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/about-open-source-europe.md): Open Source Europe is a Belgian Association that fiscally hosts over 400 initiatives and partners with philanthropic institutions to support global regranting.
- [What We Offer](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/what-we-offer.md): Open Source Europe provides the legal and financial infrastructure that open source projects need to thrive. Our services are designed to let you focus on your code, not on paperwork.
- [Umbrella organisation](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/what-we-offer/umbrella-organisation.md): Open Source Europe (OSE) provides a shared legal and financial framework for open source projects, developer networks, and software communities.
- [The Open Collective Platform](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/what-we-offer/the-open-collective-platform.md): A shared tool for managing contributions, expenses, and grants with full transparency.
- [Equivalency Determination Certificate](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/what-we-offer/equivalency-determination-certificate.md)
- [Tax-Deductible Giving for U.S. Donors](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/what-we-offer/tax-deductible-giving-for-u.s.-donors.md)
- [Terms of Service](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/terms-of-service.md)
- [How to apply](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/getting-started/how-to-apply.md)
- [Eligibility](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/getting-started/how-to-apply/eligibility.md)
- [Application Questions](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/getting-started/how-to-apply/application-questions.md)
- [Basics](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/basics.md)
- [Expenses](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/expenses.md)
- [Invoices or Reimbursements?](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/expenses/invoices-or-reimbursements.md)
- [Request a Grant (for Nonprofits Only)](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/expenses/request-a-grant-for-nonprofits-only.md): In addition to submitting an expense or invoice, the Open Collective platform also includes the option to “Request a Grant.”
- [Expenses & Getting Paid – FAQs](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/expenses/expenses-and-getting-paid-faqs.md)
- [The Partnership](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/expenses/the-partnership.md)
- [VAT in Europe and How It Works in OCE](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/expenses/the-partnership/vat-in-europe-and-how-it-works-in-oce.md)
- [Financial Contributions – How to Donate](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/financial-contributions-how-to-donate.md): Helpful guides for Donors and Financial Contributors
- [Wire Transfer Instructions via Open Collective Platform](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/financial-contributions-how-to-donate/wire-transfer-instructions-via-open-collective-platform.md): Please note that all funds have this option available.‌
- [Applying for Grants](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/financial-contributions-how-to-donate/applying-for-grants.md)
- [Fundraising Tools & Resources](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/financial-contributions-how-to-donate/fundraising-tools-and-resources.md): More ways to raise funds
- [Setting up GitHub Sponsors](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/financial-contributions-how-to-donate/setting-up-github-sponsors.md): Guide to connecting GitHub Sponsors for Organizations to your Collective
- [Contributions in cryptocurrencies](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/financial-contributions-how-to-donate/contributions-in-cryptocurrencies.md)
- [Refunds](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/how-it-works/financial-contributions-how-to-donate/refunds.md): Can I refund a donation or get a refund for one I have made?
- [Mission, Vision & Impact areas](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/about/mission-vision-and-impact-areas.md): It all comes back to resilience
- [Risk and Compliance aspects](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/about/risk-and-compliance-aspects.md): Open Collective Europe: Grant Eligibility and Risk Assessment Policy
- [Safeguarding Policy](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/about/safeguarding-policy.md)
- [Community guidelines](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/about/community-guidelines.md): These community guidelines reflects the values and commitments of Open Source Europe and aims to create a positive and productive community for all involved.
- [Terminology](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/about/terminology.md): We have developed some terminology to describe processes within the Open Collective ecosystem. Please use these terms when creating issues or support requests, instead of user —everyone is a user!
- [Contact Us](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/about/contact-us.md)
- [General FAQs](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/faq/general-faqs.md)
- [Fiscal Hosting FAQs](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/faq/fiscal-hosting-faqs.md)
- [Expense FAQs](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/faq/expense-faqs.md)
- [Contributions FAQ](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/faq/contributions-faq.md): Questions about contributing to our hosted collectives
- [Deciding on How to Use Your Money](https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/guides/deciding-on-how-to-use-your-money.md): How to use funds for your initiative on OCE


---

# Agent Instructions
This documentation is published with GitBook. GitBook is the documentation platform designed so that both humans and AI agents can read, navigate, and reason over technical content effectively. Learn more at gitbook.com.

## Querying This Documentation
If you need additional information, you can query the documentation dynamically by asking a question.
Perform an HTTP GET request on a page URL with the `ask` query parameter:
```
GET https://docs.opencollective.com/open-source-europe/master.md?ask=<question>
```
The question should be specific, self-contained, and written in natural language.
The response will contain a direct answer to the question and relevant excerpts and sources from the documentation.
Use this mechanism when the answer is not explicitly present in the current page, you need clarification or additional context, or you want to retrieve related documentation sections.
